name: Desolation Wilderness Loop
description: Classic multi-day loop through Desolation Wilderness visiting pristine alpine lakes, granite peaks, and dramatic High Sierra scenery. One of the most iconic backpacking routes in the Lake Tahoe region.
permits_required: Desolation Wilderness overnight permit required. Quota system in effect June 15 – Sept 15. Reserve at Recreation.gov
parking_info: Echo Lake trailhead parking on Johnson Pass Road. Day-use fee. Large lot fills quickly in summer.
water_availability: Numerous lakes and streams throughout the route. Water treatment required. Echo Lake, Aloha Lake, and Pyramid Lake are reliable sources.

name: Granite Chief Wilderness Traverse
description: Remote multi-day route through the rarely-visited Granite Chief Wilderness north of Lake Tahoe. Rugged terrain with outstanding views and near-complete solitude.
permits_required: Granite Chief Wilderness permit required (free, self-issue at trailhead)
parking_info: Squaw Valley base area for start. Shuttle to Barker Pass trailhead for end. Parking at both ends.
water_availability: Galena Creek and seasonal alpine streams. Dry by mid-August in some years—carry extra water.

name: Tahoe Rim Trail - Tahoe City to Echo Summit
description: Five-day segment of the Tahoe Rim Trail on the western shore offering constant lake views, meadow crossings, and high ridge walking. Accessible via multiple trailheads for shorter versions.
parking_info: Tahoe City Trailhead at 64 Acres Park. End at Echo Summit on Hwy 50. Shuttle required.
water_availability: Reliable sources at major lakes and meadow crossings. Springs may dry up late summer—check current conditions.

name: American River Gorge Backpack
description: Two-day adventure descending into the steep American River canyon through gold rush country. Camp riverside amid dramatic canyon walls and enjoy swimming holes.
parking_info: Auburn SRA trailhead off Hwy 49 near Auburn. Day-use parking fee. Multiple access points.
water_availability: North Fork American River runs year-round. Treat all water before drinking.

name: Crystal Basin High Route
description: Remote three-day traverse through the Crystal Basin linking Wrights Lake, Twin Lakes, and Loon Lake. Spectacular granite and lake scenery with excellent fishing.
permits_required: El Dorado National Forest permit required for overnight camping in designated wilderness zones
parking_info: Wrights Lake Campground trailhead. Shuttle to Loon Lake trailhead recommended.
water_availability: Abundant lakes and streams throughout route. All water requires treatment.

name: Mount Rose Wilderness Circuit
description: Two-day loop from Mount Rose Summit through the Mount Rose Wilderness with sweeping views of Lake Tahoe, Reno, and the Great Basin. Best wildflower display in early summer.
permits_required: Toiyabe National Forest overnight permit required July 4th weekend and holidays
parking_info: Mount Rose Summit trailhead on Hwy 431. Large free parking lot. Can fill on summer weekends.
water_availability: Galena Creek and seasonal alpine streams. Dry by mid-August in some years—carry extra water.

name: Meiss Country Overnight
description: Mellow introduction to backpacking in the scenic Meiss Country roadless area south of Lake Tahoe. Rolling meadows, a historic cabin, and stunning views toward the lake.
permits_required: Meiss Country Roadless Area permit not required but Leave No Trace ethics enforced
parking_info: Carson Pass trailhead on Hwy 88. Large free parking area. Popular—arrive early on weekends.
water_availability: Meiss Lake and Upper Truckee River headwaters. Seasonal—treat all water.

name: Rubicon-Rockbound Wilderness Loop
description: Challenging four-day loop linking the Desolation Wilderness and the Rockbound Valley via the famous Rubicon River canyon. A demanding but extraordinarily beautiful route.
permits_required: Desolation Wilderness overnight permit required. Self-issue quota trail heads—check Recreation.gov
parking_info: Loon Lake trailhead in Crystal Basin. Day-use fee. Parking lot fills fast in peak season.
water_availability: Rubicon River and dozens of lakes throughout. Reliable water all season but always treat.

name: North Fork American River Canyon Loop
description: Spectacular three-day loop exploring the deepest canyons of the American River drainage. Remote, rugged, and rewarding with old-growth forest and dramatic river crossings.
parking_info: Foresthill Divide trailhead. Limited parking on road shoulder. Arrive early to ensure a spot.
water_availability: North Fork American River runs strong spring through fall. Side creeks dry up by August.

name: Tamarack Lakes Backpack
description: Beginner-friendly overnight trip to the beautiful Tamarack Lakes basin in El Dorado National Forest. Perfect first backpack for scouts with straightforward trail and gorgeous alpine setting.
permits_required: No permit required. Camping setback 100 ft from water per LNT guidelines.
parking_info: Tamarack trailhead on Ice House Road off I-80. Small dirt pullout, 10-12 cars max.
water_availability: Tamarack Lakes are reliable all season. Upper lake dries to a trickle by September.

name: Bear Lake to Wentworth Springs
description: Two-day moderate route connecting Bear Lake to historic Wentworth Springs cabin site through forested Crystal Basin terrain. Great for intermediate backpackers building distance.
parking_info: Bear Lake trailhead off Ice House Rd. Small gravel lot. Shuttle to Wentworth Springs Rd recommended.
water_availability: Bear Lake outlet stream and seasonal creeks along route. Treat all sources.

name: Desolation Valley Alpine Lakes Tour
description: Three-day highlight reel of Desolation Wilderness linking Aloha Lake, Lake of the Woods, Heather Lake, and Fallen Leaf Lake. The ultimate Tahoe backpacking experience.
permits_required: Desolation Wilderness overnight permit required. Quota enforced June 15 – Sept 15.
parking_info: Start at Echo Lake trailhead (Johnson Pass Rd). Finish at Fallen Leaf Lake trailhead. Shuttle required.
water_availability: Lakes throughout route provide reliable water all season. Treat before drinking.
